markdown:
#  Update the Financials Budget Report Parameters 
##  Background 
This tutorial shows you how to update the parameters in Procore Analytics Financials Budget Report using Power BI.
##  Steps 
  1. Download a copy of your Financials Budget Report using one of these options:
     * Use the Procore Analytics Google Drive link.  
OR
     * Download a copy from Power BI. To learn how, see [Download a Report from the Power BI Service to Power BI Desktop](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-reports/service-export-to-pbix).   
  
![Power Bi Download This File](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/833422b603efa1c1e3c42a3bad610c54/d1238cc6e61edff46026c0fe862fe810/power-bi-download-this-file.png?fm=webp&h=499)  

  2. Open the file in the Microsoft Power BI Desktop application.
  3. Click **Transform Data** and choose **Edit Parameters**.   
  
![Power Bi Transform Data Edit Parameters](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/c2b6cee3032bbf033272c3babff83b1e/23fb4ad511beba081853952040f02f7f/power-bi-transform-data-edit-parameters.png?fm=webp&h=196)  

  4. In the **Edit Parameters** page, do the following:
     * **ReportName**. Keep the report name specified with the downloaded file. For example: `Procore Financials Budget`.
     * **Release Version**. Keep the version number specified with the downloaded file.
     * **Budget View Name**. Type the name of your company's budget view in this box. 
#####  Note
Procore's default budget view is the 'Procore Standard Budget View.' See [About the Procore Standard Budget ](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/about-the-procore-standard-budget-view)[View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/about-the-procore-standard-budget-view). Keep in mind that the budget views and columns in your specific budget view may be different.
       * To learn about Procore's budget views, see [What are Procore's standard budget views?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views) and [What are Procore's standard budget views for ERP Integrations?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views-for-erp-integrations)
     * **Forecast View Name**. Type the name of your company's forecasting view in this box. 
#####  Note
Procore's default forecasting view is the 'Procore Standard Forecast View.' This is an optional feature that can be turned ON/OFF in Procore projects. To learn more, see [About the Procore Standard Forecast View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/about-the-procore-standard-forecast-view).
  5. Click **Transform Data**.  
  
![Power Bi Transform Data](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/bd5571bf926f5d92221303fb9c91b212/6861fd6dd04a58faa413f5150b47923e/power-bi-transform-data.png?fm=webp&h=139)  

  6. In the **Queries** panel, click the **Budget** table. 
#####  Note
     * The steps below illustrate changes on the Budget table. However, you must perform these steps on both the **Budget** and **Budget2** queries.
     * If your company's projects are using optional Advanced Forecasting feature, you must also perform these steps on the Procore Standard Forecast View. See [About the Procore Standard Forecast View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/about-the-procore-standard-forecast-view).
  
![Power Query Editor Budget Query](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8fdb30fe48630895f2f3517b946157ad/bbc2bba5d5a8a8172ad22b83ba077cb1/power-query-editor-budget-query.png?fm=webp)
  7. In the **Query Settings** pane, under **Applied Steps** , click the gear icon next to `Expanded infoJSon`.   
  
![Power Bi Applied Steps Expanded Infojson](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/a13126d8e00b2ebdefe95a3f7e218d9f/c3e741ac944e065022424019cab6fbdb/power-bi-applied-steps-expanded-infojson.png?fm=webp)  

  8. In the **Expand infoJson** page, you have these options:
     * Add the appropriate columns by marking the appropriate checkbox.
     * Remove the appropriate budget modification columns by removing the appropriate checkbox. 
#####  Important
       * If you want to complete or have completed the migration process from budget modifications to budget changes, please read [Migrating to Budget Changes from Budget Modifications](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/migrating-to-budget-changes-from-budget-modifications) and [Add the Columns for the 'Budget Changes' Feature to a Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/add-the-columns-for-the-budget-changes-feature-to-a-budget-view).
       * If you have customized your budget view to include calculated columns, you must update those columns as appropriate. See [What are Procore's standard budget views?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views) and [What are Procore's standard budget views for ERP Integrations?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views-for-erp-integrations)
       * If you do not see a column, click **Load More** to refresh the contents of the page.
![Power Bi Expand Infojson Page](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/71e38bb6bbecd04cb26489b18271d6e6/db7ac4dbc6e0c48154b8ea032525b65a/power-bi-expand-infojson-page.png?fm=webp&h=797)
  9. In the **Query Settings** pane, under **Applied Steps** , click the X to remove `Changed Type`.  
  
![Query Settings Applied Steps Changed Type](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/c3fe3794743e8d8c81f0ffbb04d2501b/5d6cadcae1b75bbcca74add43f8081f0/query-settings-applied-steps-changed-type.png?fm=webp)
  10. Scroll through all of the columns and ensure to select the correct data type. 
#####  Tip
**How do you set the data type on a column?** Click the button next to each column header as shown below.
![Power Bi Select Data Type](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b25523b2fe7c430984bb39202801d830/c6cb787a53db5039c5ba5a2be0d40ffe/power-bi-select-data-type.png?fm=webp)  

  11. After updating all the appropriate columns and setting the data type, click **Close & Apply**.


## See Also
  * [Analytics - Report Change Log](https://v2.support.procore.com/reference-report-change-log)
  * [Analytics 1.0 - Reports](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-analytics/reports)
  * [Update a Custom Report When Migrating from Budget Modifications to Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-analytics/tutorials/update-a-custom-report-when-migrating-from-budget-modifications-to-budget-changes)
title: Update the Financials Budget Report Parameters
description: To update the parameters in the Procore Analytics Financials Budget Report to use the budget changes feature for Procore's Project level Budget Tool.
